notes: The engineering layer for BG 2.56 does not conflate commentarial traditions. Where BG 2.56 has tradition-specific readings, the engineering analog stays at the operational layer rather than picking a metaphysical side. Shankara's three-plane miseries gloss is acknowledged as commentarial expansion (the verse's literal scope is broader); the triad preserved as three distinct freedoms with engineering specifications. Ramanuja's analytic distinction (rāga as longing-for-not-yet-obtained; bhaya as affliction-from-anticipated-loss; krodha as disturbance-aimed-at-agent-of-loss) used to differentiate the three engineering pathologies — explicitly named in counter-example. The Sanskrit technical terms (where named) are preserved without being flattened to English approximations that would erase their content.
